## What this changes

<!-- Describe what this PR does. If it's a skill, name the research phase and what it produces.
If it's a methodology change, name which skill and which workflow step. -->

## Related issue

<!-- Link the issue this PR addresses. Methodology changes require an issue before a PR.
Use: Closes #123 -->

## Your positionality

<!-- What is your relationship to the research practice or methodology you're changing or adding?
Two sentences is enough. This isn't a credential — it's an accountability statement. -->

## Care check

<!-- Work through each item. If any is a no, address it before requesting review. -->

- [ ] I have read `CORE.md` and this change applies the five-principle audit throughout — not just in a preamble
- [ ] Language follows `LANGUAGE_POLICY.md`: gender-neutral defaults, no assumed pronouns, binary classifications are justified where they appear
- [ ] I have named at least one thing this change loses or compresses — the tradeoff is documented, not hidden
- [ ] I have provided at least two interpretations of the problem this solves — my approach is one reading, not the only one
- [ ] This change amplifies human judgment; it does not automate a decision that requires a person

## Downstream impact

<!-- If this changes a skill's output schema, file structure, or handoff contract:
Which downstream skills are affected? Have those handoff contracts been updated? -->

## What this does NOT do

<!-- Scope limits. What problems or cases does this explicitly not address?
This is as important as what it does. -->

## If this is a new skill

- [ ] `SKILL.md` includes: input contract, output schema, workflow, quality gates, pipeline position, and scope limits
- [ ] `REFERENCE.md` exists and covers edge cases
- [ ] `EXAMPLES.md` exists with at least one worked example showing the care check in action
- [ ] A slash command file exists in `.claude/commands/`
- [ ] The skill has been tested with a real or realistic research scenario (describe briefly below)

**Test scenario:**
